title: AGENT-CLI.md frontmatter — agentcli-interactive/v1
description: JSON Schema for the YAML frontmatter of an AIP-45 AGENT-CLI.md manifest. Sibling of AIP-29 (CLI.md, tool CLIs); covers interactive agent CLIs (Hermes, Claude Code, OpenCode, Goose, Gemini CLI). Shares install/version/auth blocks with AIP-29 via $ref; binds to AIP-44 ACP for the wire protocol when protocol=acp.

name.description: Kebab id, MUST equal the parent directory name.

bin.description: Path to the binary (absolute or PATH-resolvable).

install.description: Install methods — shared with AIP-29 via $ref.

setup.description: Post-install configuration steps — shared with AIP-29 via $ref. Used for sidecar daemons (openclaw onboard) and one-time bind config (gateway URL, default channel) that don't fit the auth state machine.

sandbox.description: Inherits AIP-36 SANDBOX.md verbatim. Either an inline block or a workspace-relative ref to a SANDBOX.md.

protocol.description: Wire protocol discriminator. acp = AIP-44 ACP server; mcp = MCP-over-stdio; proprietary = adapter package implements AgentCliClient.

acp.description: Workspace-relative ref to an AIP-44 ACP.md describing the wire profile. REQUIRED when protocol=acp.

mcp.description: MCP server config. REQUIRED when protocol=mcp.

adapter.description: NPM package name implementing AgentCliClient. REQUIRED when protocol=proprietary.

modes.description: Mutually-exclusive operation modes the CLI exposes. The host picks at most one per turn via OPERATOR.runtime.config.mode. Each mode patches bin_args / env; the patches are applied AFTER the manifest's default bin_args and BEFORE option patches. Common shape: claude-code's permission modes (default | plan | accept-edits | bypass-permissions).

options.description: Independent typed knobs the CLI accepts. The host picks any subset per turn via OPERATOR.runtime.config.options. Patches apply in declaration order, AFTER mode patches.

mode.description: ephemeral = torn down on disconnect; persistent = survives reconnect within idle_timeout_ms; resumable = durable across runs (requires capabilities.resumable: true).

models.description: Model routing for the agent. Maps logical defaults to env var slots resolved via AIP-19 SECRETS.md.

default.description: Default model id passed to the agent.

allowed.description: Model ids the host MAY route to.

env.description: Map of model slot -> env var name (e.g. anthropic -> ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).

capabilities.description: Manifest-declared capabilities. When protocol=acp, MUST be a subset of the AIP-44 ACP.md capabilities the bound runtime negotiates.

mode.description: A single mutually-exclusive operation mode. Exactly one mode is active per turn; modes do not stack. Use options for independent knobs.

id.description: Stable id. The operator config references this id.

bin_args_append.description: Extra argv appended after the manifest's bin_args when this mode is active.

env.description: Extra env vars merged into the spawn env when this mode is active.

option.description: A single typed knob. Multiple options can be active in the same turn; each option's patch is independent.

id.description: Stable id. The operator config references this id.

default.description: Default value used when the operator config omits this option. Type matches the declared `type`.

bin_args_template.description: Argv template appended when the option has a non-default value. The literal token `{value}` is replaced with the option's value (stringified). Use for value-bearing flags like `--model {value}` or `--max-turns {value}`.

bin_args_append_when_true.description: Argv appended when type=boolean AND value=true. Use for bare flags like `--auto`.

env.description: Env vars to merge when the option has a non-default value. Values may contain `{value}` for templating.

continuation.description: How prior turns reach the CLI. The driver hosts a strategy registry; this block declares which strategies fit this CLI and which is the default. The operator config MAY override `default` with any id from `supported`.

supported.description: Strategies the CLI is known to work with. The host MUST refuse to run with a strategy outside this list.

pinned_session.description: Tuning for the pinned-session strategy. Ignored by other strategies.

idle_timeout_ms.description: How long an idle pinned session is kept alive before the driver closes it. Applies in addition to the CLI's own session.idle_timeout_ms.

key_scope.description: Composite key that uniquely identifies a pinned session. Default keys by (conversation, operator) — different conversations get different child processes; one operator per conversation reuses one process across turns.

continuationStrategyId.description: Built-in strategy ids. Future custom strategies require a follow-up AIP that opens this enum.
